behavior_summary: This script implements the NPC 'Beren' behavior, including combat entry, theft reaction, scheduling, fast-area reactions, and a multi-stage conversation system with menu-driven dialogue and temperamental state.
U8P_BEREN::cast (process) is the primary combat entry: if the NPC is on map 0x29 it treats the action as a crime (calls guard response); otherwise it sets berenMad and enters a combat/spell AI loop.

U8P_BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething (process) handles nearby thefts: verifies vertical proximity to the stolen object, resets internal references, increments an escalating warning counter (berenWarned), issues progressively sterner barks, may immobilize the avatar briefly, call guards, or set the NPC target to attack.
U8P_BEREN::look (process) provides context-sensitive short descriptions depending on flags (berenName, berenJob) and whether the NPC is dead.

U8P_BEREN::schedule (process) chooses a U8P_BEREN::schedule/movement plan based on map, pyrosFree and time-of-day; it asks a helper (METHOD::func143A), tests the result and writes berenSchedule when a choice is committed.
U8P_BEREN::enterFastArea / U8P_BEREN::leaveFastArea are area entry/exit triggers: entering can set targets or trigger quick movement; leaving clears fast-area references (FREE::resetRef).

U8P_BEREN::use (process) is the interaction entry: opens/closes gumps, begins conversation and dispatches into several dialogue handlers (U8P_BEREN::func0862, ::func1FD1, ::func31C2, ::func369B, ::func3856) depending on flags and context.

U8P_BEREN::func3856 and U8P_BEREN::func1FD1 are the main menu-driven conversation cores, building MenuSet/MenuUnion/MenuRemove lists, looping Ask prompts, capturing choices into locals (menu_choice variables) and branching into temper/forgive/attack flows (including calls to U8P_BEREN::func1296 and U8P_BEREN::func1A56).
notable_control_flow: U8P_BEREN::cast: check Npc::getMap(this) == 0x29 -> CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime (suspend) else set berenMad=0x03 -> SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop (suspend).

U8P_BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething: validate param1 and Z-distance (Item::getZ) -> FREE::resetRef + METHOD::func091C -> suspend -> branch by map and berenWarned to escalate warning barks, call guards or set target and setAvatarInStasis.

U8P_BEREN::schedule: skip if dead or inDaemonScene; choose branch by Npc::getMap and pyrosFree, compute local2/local1/local0, call METHOD::func143A and on process_result() success set berenSchedule then call METHOD::func133F.

U8P_BEREN::use: if NPC dead toggle gump via Item::getStatus/Item::openGump/Item::closeGump; BeginConversation -> call appropriate dialogue helper based on acolyteKilled, berenMad, map checks and target isDead checks -> EndConversation.

Conversation loops (U8P_BEREN::func3856/U8P_BEREN::func1FD1): assemble menu lists, move 'Farewell.' to end, ensure list length > 1, Ask -> SuspendAssign into local menu_choice variables -> strcmp branches to route to specific responses and adjust flags (berenName, berenMad, berenAvaName, startedQuestions/finishedQuestions).
open_questions: What exact data type is the U8P_BEREN::cast/U8P_BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething parameter (uword param1)? It appears to be an item or actor reference but this is inferred (confidence: medium).

What does METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func143A / METHOD::func133F concretely do? They appear to be bookkeeping/movement/scheduling helpers but their side effects are only inferred (confidence: low/medium).

Some internal function names referenced in calls (e.g., BEREN::func06E5) are present in call lists but not available as full definitions here; the role and effect of those are uncertain (confidence: low).

Exact numeric meanings of berenMad values (0x01..0x03) are inferred as tone/anger levels; mapping to in-game states (e.g., friendly/annoyed/hostile) is plausible but not fully enumerated (confidence: medium).

main_function.name: U8P_BEREN::cast
main_function.purpose: Primary combat/U8P_BEREN::cast entry point: decide whether the action should be treated as a crime (map 0x29) or as hostile combat, set anger state and enter combat AI.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when this NPC's 'U8P_BEREN::cast' behavior/process triggers (process signature: process U8P_BEREN::cast(uword)).
Parameter param1 is passed in but not used for branching in the main path (appears to be context/item reference in other callers).

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::cast
function_map.role: Combat/behavior entry: branch on map for guard response or set berenMad and start combat/spell loop.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Process theft reactions: validate stolen object proximity, reset refs, increment berenWarned, bark/escalate, call guards or force attack/stasis.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::look
function_map.role: Provide U8P_BEREN::look/examine text variants depending on berenName/berenJob and death state.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::schedule
function_map.role: NPC scheduling: choose movement/U8P_BEREN::schedule based on map, pyrosFree and time-of-day and commit via METHOD helpers.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: set NPC target or call quick-move helper to react to nearby actor entry.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: clear fast-area reference via FREE::resetRef.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::use
function_map.role: Player 'U8P_BEREN::use' interaction entry: open/close gumps, begin conversation and dispatch to dialogue helpers based on flags/context.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::func0862
function_map.role: Short dialogue/menu routine for first-meeting or small greetings; captures player-provided name and sets bergen-related flags.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::func1296
function_map.role: Short randomized monologue / temperamental bark bucket (insults/anger variants).

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::func1A56
function_map.role: Randomized closing / farewell monologue bucket, modulated by berenMad and berenAvaName.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::func1FD1
function_map.role: Menu-driven dialogue handler that assembles/augments menus, captures choices, and routes to forgiveness/insult/attack branches.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::func31C2
function_map.role: Lore/roleplay monologue chooser; handles 'acolyteKilled' grief branch and increments tickedOff.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::func369B
function_map.role: Short deferential reply; gates longer grovelling line via berenGroveled flag and calls bookkeeping helper METHOD::func15E7.

function_map.name: U8P_BEREN::func3856
function_map.role: Primary complex conversation core: builds and mutates menus, Ask loops, captures multiple choices and drives most player-facing dialog branches.

internal_calls.purpose: Route to the short greeting/first-meeting dialog when acolyteKilled == 0.
Enter mid-sized/angry dialogue when berenMad is set.
Call lore/roleplay monologue when target actor is alive.
Fallback polite/deferential reply when other branches don't apply and target is dead or context requires it.
Route into the main complex conversation when acolyteKilled != 0 and other conditions (default richer dialog).
When the player chooses conciliatory options, escalate into the main conversation handler.
Invoke short temperamental monologue as an angry response branch.
Invoke temperamental response in conversation branches.
Invoke closing/farewell monologue in certain conversation branches.
Referenced bookkeeping / Beren-specific helper called during theft response (definition not present in this sheet).

external_calls.purpose: Alert guards / treat action as a crime when NPC is on map 0x29.
Enter combat/spell AI loop after setting berenMad (start hostile behavior).
Clear stored fast-area or stolen-item reference before escalation.
Compare vertical positions to verify stolen item proximity before executing theft reaction.
Internal bookkeeping called during theft handling (purpose inferred).
Assign NPC aggro/attack target when escalating to direct aggression.
Temporarily immobilize the avatar as part of the theft reaction.
Request/apply a candidate U8P_BEREN::schedule/movement option; result is tested by process_result().
Post-selection finalization after a U8P_BEREN::schedule is accepted (commit or follow-up action).
Open the NPC/item gump when the player interacts and conditions permit.
Close the gump on certain dead-state interactions.
Check menu length to ensure there are choices before asking; repopulate if needed.
Reorder menu so 'Farewell.' appears last prior to Ask.
Retrieve the player's name to compose personalized reply when the avatar provides their name.
Bookkeeping/interaction calls surrounding the deferential reply (purpose inferred).

reads.name: berenName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Beren uses name-based or full-name title variants in U8P_BEREN::look and dialogue branches.

reads.name: berenJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls U8P_BEREN::use of job/title wording in U8P_BEREN::look and some dialogue options.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Used by U8P_BEREN::schedule() together with map to pick U8P_BEREN::schedule branches when on map 0x29.

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Short-circuits scheduling and some fast-area behavior when set.

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Switches certain dialogue/monologue branches into grief/vengeful responses.

reads.name: berenMad
reads.likely_effect: Represents NPC anger/hostility level read by dialogue/speech routines to change tone and by U8P_BEREN::use/U8P_BEREN::cast to route to hostility behavior.

reads.name: berenAvaName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Beren addresses the avatar by name (getName) in dialogues.

reads.name: startedQuestions
reads.likely_effect: Used to resume or alter a question thread in U8P_BEREN::func3856 conversation assembly.

reads.name: finishedQuestions
reads.likely_effect: Used with startedQuestions to gate resumed-question branches.

reads.name: tickedOff
reads.likely_effect: Tracks how many times Beren has issued angry lines in response to acolyteKilled events; used to escalate barks and set target.

writes.name: berenMad
writes.likely_effect: Set to 0x03 by U8P_BEREN::cast() to mark NPC as fully enraged/hostile before entering combat AI.

writes.name: berenWarned
writes.likely_effect: Incremented by U8P_BEREN::AvatarStoleSomething to track theft warnings and escalate behavior (guards/attack) as warnings accumulate.

writes.name: berenSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_BEREN::schedule() to record the chosen U8P_BEREN::schedule variant after METHOD::func143A succeeds.

writes.name: berenMet
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player first meets Beren (used to avoid repeating name-capture dialog).

writes.name: berenAvaName
writes.likely_effect: Set when the avatar supplies their name in U8P_BEREN::func0862, enabling personalized dialogue later.

writes.name: berenGroveled
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_BEREN::func369B after a long deferential line to avoid repeating the grovel; acts as a one-time gating flag.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the NPC 'Beren' behavior, including combat entry, theft reaction, scheduling, fast-area reactions, and a multi-stage conversation system with menu-driven dialogue and temperamental state.
